column1: # Tests that longer and narrower type signatures match first.
# Tests arguments split over the stack and future tokens.
# 1-arg: integer on stack, prefix (via math module)
# 2-arg forward: left from stack, right collected from forward
# --- Narrower type wins over wider type ---
# Two integers should use integer addition, not string concatenation
# Two strings should use scalar/string concatenation
# Mixed: integer + string → scalar sig (string concat)
# --- Stack split: some args from stack, some from forward ---
# 1 already on stack, add collects 2 from forward
# Stack fully satisfies add's sig — no forward collection needed.
# add forward-collects 3, takes 2 from stack. 1 remains.
1 add 2 mul 3 add 4 mul 5 sub 6 add 7
# --- Forward precedence with varying arg counts ---
# add takes 2 args (forward precedence), 1 from stack + 1 forward
def add5 [add 5] end def mul2 [mul 2] end 3 add5 mul2
# Words that don't consume everything leave stack values
# boolean/true is more specific than boolean
# For boolean args, the boolean sig wins (registered first at equal score)
# --- Suffix with all args from future (no prefix args) ---
# Fully forward: forward args fill sigArgs[0] first, swap convention applies
